title: calliandra viscidula
intro: Calliandra viscidula, also known as the "pink powder puff," is a flowering plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its clusters of small, pink or red flowers and sticky leaves. The medicinal properties of Calliandra viscidula include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Calliandra viscidula can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to harness its medicinal properties.
